observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive, as the primarily responsible official, to direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the CY 2024 year-end financial statements and supporting schedules to allow ample time for the audit team’s review. Henceforth, implement necessary measures to ensure the submission of the required year-end financial statements within the prescribed period.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the MTO to:
Record and report checks in the RCI only upon actual issuance or release to the claimants, in accordance with the MNGAS guidelines;
Ensure that the RCIs are submitted to the MACCO together with the complete set of check DVs as reported; and
Establish and enforce strong internal controls to ensure that claimants sign the check register to acknowledge receipt of the checks.
Direct the MACCO and MTO to coordinate to ensure that checks that remain unreleased at year-end are not recorded as disbursements and are instead recognized under the appropriate payable accounts, in compliance with proper accounting standards.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the MSWDO to:
Immediately execute contracts with all beneficiaries of the SEA-Kaunlaran project to establish the legal basis for the recovery of the capital assistance and to ensure the sustainability of the municipal government’s project.
Collect the capital assistances that are already due for repayment that may be used as funds for future beneficiaries.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the capital assistance as receivables upon execution of the contracts, in accordance with accounting standards, and ensure proper monitoring of repayments for accurate financial reporting. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that contingent assets are properly disclosed in the financial statements.

We recommended to the management to:
a.	Submit immediately to the audit team the enumerated lacking documents to complete our post audit. Secure documents from the LMP-Bukidnon Chapter regarding the breakdown of paid registration fees to accurately account for the same, including the pertinent documents necessary for the proper liquidation thereof. Non-submission may warrant the issuance of an audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.
b.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to, henceforth:
1.	Ensure that payments for “registration fees” for purposes directly related to travel expenses should be treated as cash advances, subject to proper documentation and liquidation; and
2.	Ensure that expenses related to foreign travel are recorded to the Travelling Expenses – Foreign account.
c.	Ensure that every disbursement of government funds is safeguarded against illegal or improper disposition and that the funds are utilized efficiently, economically, and effectively.
We recommended to the management to, henceforth, stop the practice of granting subsidies or any forms of payments to PBs without any legal basis.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant and the MSWDO to:
Submit a justification for granting subsidies to DAWFED and PWD Federation Presidents in the form of an allowance without requiring sufficient documentation to support the proper utilization of funds.
Submit a copy of the DAWFED minutes of meetings for the year 2024.
Provide an explanation for the payment of subsidies despite the recipients’ failure to attend the meeting, and clarify the discrepancies noted in the attendance or absences during the fifth meeting.
Henceforth, stop the practice of granting subsidies to DAWFED and PWD federation presidents in the form of allowance. Instead, disbursements for meals should go through the required procurement process and supported with complete documents. Likewise, travel expenses should also be supported with complete documents.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the required documents in Annex H to complete the audit team’s post audit of the disbursements on or before the end of April 2025, otherwise, non-submission may warrant the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.
Henceforth, ensure the following for every payment of financial assistance or subsidy:
If related to AICS, it must adhere to the LGU’s established guidelines by supporting the disbursement with the required documents and observing the established threshold; otherwise, deviations from the guidelines must be justified with relevant documentation;
It must be supported with documents clearly stating the purpose;
If related to LGU activities, it must be supported with complete documents related to its procurement and/or utilization, if applicable; otherwise, it must be supported with justification and other relevant documents.
For financial assistance to barangay or other government agencies, payment should be made to the name of the barangay or agency and duly supported with an official receipt. Moreover, the payment should be subject for liquidation.
For financial assistance or subsidy to various individuals through payroll, it must be supported with documents indicating the reason for their designation as payees and other relevant supporting documents.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the concerned personnel to submit the required documents detailed in Annex I to establish the legality, validity, propriety, and regularity of the disbursements, particularly:
Travel authorization or justification for personnel not included in the duly approved travel order.
Justification for certificates of appearance issued by establishments instead of the head of agency/department conducting the activity.
Justification for registration fees being supported with receipts issued by establishments.
Justification for registration fees being supported with receipts issued by leagues without supporting communication letters from the concerned leagues.
Non-submission of the documents may warrant the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry for the erroneous recording of expense in JEV Nos. 02-551, 03-1233, and 03-1244, which resulted to overstatement of expenses aggregating P29,600.00.
We further recommended to the management to, henceforth:
Remind all departments that activities such as workshops or assessments should be held within municipal premises unless justified by compelling reasons. Require future activity designs or equivalent documentation to include clear justification for the choice of venue, especially if outside the province or region.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs by ensuring that traveling expenses and registration fees are recorded under the correct expense accounts. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that disbursements for travel are supported with proper documentation to establish their legality, validity, propriety, and regularity.

We recommended to the management to, henceforth:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Ensure that accountable forms issued to collectors are designated by fund per stub—thus, one stub should be used exclusively for GF collections, and another stub for TF collections (except for AF No. 56, which is divided equally between GF and SEF, making it unnecessary to separate stubs for each fund).
Instruct all AOs in the MTO to prepare RCDs by fund, ensuring that Section C (RAAF) of the RCD includes only the accountable forms specific to that fund.
Ensure that accountable forms are properly attached to the specific RCD to which they pertain.
Ensure that cashbooks maintained by all AOs are segregated by fund—one cashbook to record GF collections only, one for SEF, and one for TF. Moreover, ensure that all collections and remittances are completely and accurately recorded in the cashbooks, with monthly totals and certifications. Additionally, avoid tampering with the cashbooks by pasting certifications; instead, AOs may opt to use stamps for their certifications.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure that GF JEVs are supported with GF RCD and supporting accountable forms and deposit slips, SEF JEVs are supported with SEF documents, and TF JEVs with TF documents.
Ensure that JEVs for collections not deposited in the same month are supported with the related RCD and accountable forms. In the succeeding month’s JEV recording the deposit, the same RCD should also be attached along with the applicable deposit slip.

We recommended to the management to direct the HRMO to submit the following:
Any document stating the specific tasks/projects performed by the personnel and the period covered, duly certified by the head of office, for all payments to COS and EH personnel in CY 2024.
Letter justification for the handwritten DTRs.
Additional documents for payments supported with payroll only.
Non-submission of the above documents may warrant the issuance of audit suspension.
We further recommended to the management to direct the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to, henceforth, ensure the following:
Personnel classifications, particularly for COS and EH, are in accordance with COA-DBM Joint Circular to prevent any confusion regarding the proper treatment of the transactions and the corresponding supporting documents.
All personnel classified as JOs under the Joint Circular, regardless of management’s internal classifications, are required to prepare accomplishment reports duly verified by the head of office.
For personnel assigned to remote locations, those with irregular schedules, or those hired for a short duration, payments of wages may be supported with:
a. A “Certificate of Service”, duly approved by the Head of Office, instead of handwritten DTRs, which may not accurately reflect the actual time rendered, particularly for those who are on a fixed-rate basis.
b. A Justification for handwritten DTR, duly approved by the Head of Office; the DTR should reflect actual log in and out of the personnel, particularly for those who are on a daily-rate basis.
The Job Order, or other similar documents, should indicate the nature and other agreed-upon terms between the LGU and the personnel, such as whether they are on a daily-rate or fixed-rate basis, whether the schedule is irregular, whether DTRs are not applicable, whether the contract period is fixed or extendable, and such other necessary information to establish the necessity, validity, and propriety of the payments to personnel.
For payments to individuals other than JOs, such as barangay workers/volunteers, supporting documents should include:
Certification that the individuals are entitled to such payment or any document evidencing performance of the duties required for the payment or as required by applicable rules and regulations.
Other pertinent supporting documents as are required by the nature of expense.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the BAC to submit an explanation for the non-adherence to the procurement process for the disbursements shown in Annex K.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit any documents evidencing that the performers/artists actually performed, such as certifications and pictures, to support the validity and propriety of the disbursements.
Non-submission of the above documents may warrant the issuance of audit suspension.
We further recommended to the management to direct the BAC and Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments to performers, entertainers, and other similar types of artistic work are henceforth supported with the following:
Documentation of the market study conducted to determine the probable sources and confirm that the supplier or contractor could undertake the project at more advantageous terms.
Notice of Award, once the BAC had a successful negotiation with the supplier or contractor, duly approved by the HOPE.
Contract between the LGU and supplier or contractor, detailing the necessary terms and conditions.
Notice to Proceed.
Certificate of completion or acceptance, pictures during the activity, pictures of the completed output (particularly for sculptors and similar works), and other relevant supporting documents to establish the validity and propriety of the disbursement.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the lacking documents, particularly:
The distribution sheet for JEV No. 0090 and 0440, which should be completely signed by the intended recipients; and
The attendance sheet for JEV No. 0095.
Justification on the difference of the number participants in the attendance sheet compared to the number of meals/snacks procured in JEV No. 2155 and 1662. Also, provide justification on the duplicate signatured in JEV No. 3196.
Henceforth, ensure that the attendance sheets, distribution sheets and activity designs attached to the disbursement vouchers for meals and food supplies are reliable, acceptable and satisfactory:
Attendance sheets should clearly indicate that all meals and snacks were provided to the participants. The number of participants should not vary from the number of purchased meals and snacks; otherwise, the office in charge must provide a justification, which should be attached to the disbursement voucher;
Distribution sheets should clearly indicate the quantity and food items distributed to each recipient. The total quantity distributed should tally with the procured food items; otherwise, the office in charge must provide a justification, or any undistributed food items are properly returned to the LGU’s inventory;
For purchases that include raw food items, the activity designs should specify the intended menu to ensure proper documentation; and
Attendance sheets should not contain duplicate signatures to maintain the integrity of the document.

We recommended to the management to direct the BAC to, henceforth, strictly adhere to the aforementioned guidelines by ensuring that the specific descriptions of meals and snacks be indicated in the procurement documents. This will enhance accuracy and competitiveness in the suppliers’ quotations, ensure clarity in the agreed-upon terms for delivery (particularly in the PO), improve process efficiency, and preserve the integrity of the procurement process.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Immediately submit the journal entry vouchers together with the source and supporting documents for post-audit and verification in order to avoid any effects on the auditor’s opinion on the financial statements.
Henceforth, ensure that complete JEVs and supporting documents, for all types of transactions, including the listed transactions in the General Journals, are completely forwarded to COA for post-audit and verification.
observations.reason:
1.	GJ JEVs and source documents for the years 2022-2023 are not yet completely submitted.
2.	For 2024, still not practiced the monthly submission of GJ JEVs and its source documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:
Implement COA Circular 2020-006 since it will solve various issues involving PPE accounts of the agency, including the issue of the retention in the Trust Fund account the PPEs amounting to 3.7 Million.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to to review the correctness of the reported accumulated depreciation and the carrying value as of December 31, 2023. Also, review the correctness and completeness of the other factors affecting the computation of depreciation expenses, such as the acquisition date, the computed age of each PPE, the remaining useful life, and the formula used.
As to the “Local Road Network” account:
direct the municipal engineer to break the costs of the local road network according to its components and provide the same to the municipal accountant and the general services officer to aid proper recording and reporting of the account.
direct the Municipal Accountant to recompute, on a per-component basis, the accumulated depreciation as of December 31, 2023, for each of the local road networks, make the necessary adjusting entries, and submit the related Journal Entry Voucher(s), the detailed recomputation, and other supporting documents to the audit team for verification.
observations.reason:
1.	One-time cleansing not yet implemented due to lacking personnel to perform the task.
2.	Adjustments for accumulated depreciation for review by the audit team.
3.	Ongoing matching with MACCO and GSO records

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to either:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: 1) ensure that receipts of intra-agency fund transfers (through checks) are duly acknowledged through the issuance of an official receipt; and 2) record the collection in the Cash in Treasury cashbook. Also instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all collections received by the Municipal Treasurer are properly recorded in the “Cash-Local Treasury” account; or
Utilize other facilities of the AGDB, either the auto-debit facility or the weAccess facility of the bank, for intra-agency fund transfers.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” and make necessary adjustments to correct the balances of said accounts. Further, submit the supporting documents of the JEVs mentioned in Annexes I and complete the supporting documents of the transactions mentioned in Annex J to complete post-audit and verification.
Henceforth, ensure submission of supporting documents to JEVs initially recording in the General Journal and ensure that supporting documents attached will render enough explanation and information to the audit team to aid post-audit and verification of each of the transactions and would warrant the necessity, validity, propriety, and regularity of the transactions of the LGU.
observations.reason:
1.	Submitted documents were not complete
2.	Incomplete submission of GJ JEVs for CY 2024

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to verify the status of the recorded stale checks to see if the payee still has valid claims. Additionally, the Municipal Accountant should immediately prepare adjusting entries for the stale check that has valid claims by debiting “Prior Period Adjustment” and crediting “Account Payable” accounts and submit the JEV along with the confirmation letter of the payee to the office of the auditor for review. Henceforth, record stale checks in accordance with Section 61, Item 6, of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the concerned officials to submit the lacking certifications.
Direct the HRMO and the Municipal Accountant to recompute the payment of TA for CY 2023 with considerations to the submitted certifications by the concerned officials.  Overpayments, if there are any, should be therefore be restituted to the funds of the LGU. Also, submit to the audit team copy of the working paper of the recomputation and the complete supporting documents shall be thereto attached.
Henceforth, to choose one from the following:
Assign vehicle to the head of office but not to pay the commutable TA;
Pay the commutable TA but will not assign vehicle to the head of office; or
Pay the commutable TA but with corresponding deduction if the head of office used a government vehicle; and
Henceforth, ensure that the payment for RATA is supported with complete prescribed documents.

We recommended to the Management to direct:
all heads of officers to prepare “Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMP)” with recommendations of the mode of procurement and submit the same to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation into the Annual Procurement Plan,
the BAC Secretariat to consolidate thereafter the submitted PPMP from the offices to Annual Procurement Plan with final recommendations of the appropriate procurement modality, that is, as much as possible, through public bidding.
the GSO to prepare a PR on a quarterly basis based on the approved APP.
observations.reason: Procurement still not done on a quarterly basis

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the remittance of the unutilized collection from the sale of bid documents and liquidated damages in CY 2023, totaling ₱37,161.86 in the General Fund.

Henceforth, record the proceeds from the sale of bid documents and liquidated damages in the "Trust Liabilities" account and remit to the General Fund the unutilized collection from the sale of bid documents and liquidated damages at the end of the year.

We recommended the management to stop the improper practice of granting BAC honoraria in excess of the 25% basic monthly salary and honorarium for BAC Support staff. Moreover, the management may consider granting overtime pay for the latter if warranted.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts in the books of the LGU. Moreover, review the prior year projects related to road rehabilitation/maintenance recorded as assets and adjust the same duly supported with working papers showing all the details/information particularly the actual scope of works and pictures, to also support the claim that only maintenance works were undertaken on the projects.

We further recommended to the management that thereafter, all future projects that qualify as regular maintenance shall be directly recorded as an expense account for the rehabilitation/maintenance of road projects.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Provide a reconciliation statement of the noted variance in the beginning and ending balances of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account;
Require the barangays to liquidate the financial assistance as soon as possible, and
Henceforth, ensure that the required details, as well as other relevant information and transactions, of the LDRRMF are disclosed in the notes to the financial statements.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to ensure compliance to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 by taking the following steps:
a. Facilitate the preparation and submission of photocopies of previous payrolls for the same projects, SLs, and the submission of certification of payment received for payrolls with unoriginal signatures, to complete the post-audit of the disbursements;
b. Provide justification, or submit corrected documents, if necessary, for documents with inconsistencies or other deficiencies; and
c. Ensure that all subsequent labor disbursements for construction are supported with complete documentation. In addition to the usual supporting documents, include inspection reports indicating current status of project, photocopies of previous payrolls, and SLs.
observations.reason: Required documents still not completely submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to implement the use of the perpetual inventory method in all regular purchases of supplies and materials, designate a specific place to be used as storage for the inventory items and the GSO to maintain a Stock Card for each kind of item.

We further recommended to the management to direct:

1)	The GSO to ensure that every issuance of supplies and materials is supported with RIS. All RIS are consolidated weekly using the SSMI and submitted to the Accounting Unit for recording of expenses;

2)	The Municipal Accountant to record procured supplies and materials by debiting the corresponding Inventory Accounts under the perpetual inventory method in compliance with the NGAS Manual for LGUs, and to ensure that the RIS and/or SSMI are used to support the recording of expenses.

observations.reason: No compliance to the required justification (No.1) and to the submission of lacking supporting documents marked “X” (No.2)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to direct the concerned offices/personnel to:
a)	Provide explanation/justification for the following:
1.	discrepancies in items and quantities in the PR, PO, AIR, and charge invoices,
2.	why the attached charge invoices are not dated,
3.	erasures and superimpositions in the dates, and
4.	travel purposes not directly related to the budget item it was charged to;
b)	Submit the following documents:
1.	lacking charge invoice and corresponding trip ticket and consumption reports for JEV no. 01-0231 and 12-5235,
2.	the POW and detailed estimates and corresponding approved APP for every fuel disbursement in Annex H, and
3.	approved fuel consumption reports for the consumption reports which were not signed approved (refer to Annex I);
c)	Ensure that, for subsequent fuel disbursements, documents are printed to ensure their readability, if not, ensure that the handwriting is legible;
d)	Require a summary list or a statement of account from the supplier prior to every disbursement, and
e)	Ensure that accrual accounting is observed by promptly processing and recording expenses in the year they were incurred, regardless of payment.
observations.reason: No compliance to the required justification on the noted discrepancies; and no submission of lacking documents (charge invoice, trip tickets and consumption reports, POW and APP)

1.	No submission of the required documents. 
2.	Filling out of PO still not done properly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:
a)	Submit sufficient documentation to support the grant of time extension to the contractors, or provide justification to the grant of time extension with insufficient details and supporting documentation for the unfavorable weather condition, otherwise, require the contractors to pay for LD;
b)	Justify the time extension granted for election ban when a Certificate of Exception issued by the COMELEC was obtained for the two aforementioned projects; and
c)	Instruct concerned personnel to conduct regular monitoring and inspection of ongoing projects to ensure that they are being completed on time and in accordance with the contract, and to ensure that LDs are imposed on contractors that fail to complete the projects on time with insufficient grounds and supporting documentation in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Submitted documents were insufficient to support the grant of time extension.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Head of the Agency to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to reclassify the completed project recorded under “Construction in Progress” account to its proper infrastructure asset account. Henceforth, ensure timely reclassification of transactions with similar nature herewith.
observations.reason: Incomplete reclassification of the completed projects recorded under the “Construction in Progress” account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to then Management to instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to immediately correct the accounting errors and henceforth compute the depreciation in accordance with the provisions of numbers 12 and 14 of Item IV of COA Circular No. 2015-008,
Municipal Engineer to periodically provide the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects as basis of the latter to update their respective records/inventory,
c. MGSO to maintain and periodically update the Municipal Government’s Local Road Inventory and Road Map and include the value of the road lot components provided by the Municipal Assessor, and
d. Henceforth, the MGSO to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of their records as regards Road Networks on or before the end of each year.
observations.reason: Incomplete Inventory of Local Roads and Road Map; Incomplete reconciliation between MGSO and Municipal Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as required in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.  Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB through the GPPB website.
observations.reason: Still not implemented, lacking staff to comply recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management, particularly the Municipal Accounting Office, to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) supported with working papers and necessary documents, in order to adjust the accounts "CIP" and "AP" to the correct amount and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor. We further recommended to the Offices of the Municipal Accounting and Engineering to have close coordination regarding the status of all on-going and completed projects particularly at year end.
observations.reason: No adjustment made in the year-end of CY 2024
Non-maintenance of stock cards.
Still no breakdown of the “Other Receivables” and “Advances to Officers and Employees” account. Suggested to be requested for write-off of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to require the following offices to:

A. General Services Office:
•  Register all unregistered land under the Torrens Title System with the Local Registration Authority- Registry of Deeds, in accordance with the provisions of Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;
•   Ensure custody of all land titles of the Municipality by establishing an archival system to ensure the safety and protection of all government property, public documents or records such as property inventory report, land ownership, and such other records or documents of public interest in the various offices and departments in the municipality.

B. Municipal Accounting Office:
• Determine the original entry in recording the land acquired through purchase and secure supporting documents pertaining thereto, including those acquired through donation, and effect necessary adjustments in the books of accounts;

C. Municipal Assessors Office:
• Communicate with the Land Registration Authority/Registry of Deeds and secure a copy of the list of lands or titles, if possible, belonging to the Municipality, and reconcile results thereof with the Land recorded in the books of accounts and in the ledger card maintained by General Services Office; Investigate discrepancies, if any, and communicate with the Accounting Office and General Services Office noted discrepancies to facilitate adjustments in the books of accounts and/or ledger cards.
observations.reason: Still on-going processing of land titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards, respectively, for each category plant, property and equipment and to coordinate with each other on a monthly basis to reconcile the records of both offices.
observations.reason: On-going setting up of land items on individual subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the agency shall conduct complete inventory of its PPEs, reconcile the result of the count with the accounting records, and to report the results of the count and reconciliation using the RPCPPE, furnishing a copy thereof to the Auditor. The cause/s of overage/shortage, if any, shall be investigated also and shall hold the custodian of the shortage property accountable.
Further, require the Accounting Office to establish the details of those described as “beginning balances’ of the items of property, identify and specify the accurate description of the property items categorized as fully depreciated assets, and to exert efforts to establish the other categorized unaccounted property especially the material amount in Construction and Heavy Equipment in General Fund.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall issue demand letters and require the concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances. Keep the granting and utilization of cash advances strictly within the prescriptions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 and stop granting excessive cash advances.
observations.reason: There were still prior years unliquidated cash advances that were still not liquidated as of December 31, 2024. Suggested to be requested for write-off.

Control and monitoring of fuel consumption should be made in order to ascertain the validity and propriety on the expenses incurred on fuel, oil and gasoline and to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures of fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 39-41 | We are updating to your end that submission of monthly fuel consumption report is operative last February 2017. We just situate Logo and For official use only marker on our vehicle last February 28, 2017 attached pictures of vehicles.

We recommended that management religiously observe Section 2.2 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Fully ensure that all projects are included in the Annual and Supplemental Investment Program to fully account what the LGU has planned with regards to utilization of 20% LDF. Finally, involve the municipal development council in the identification of priority development projects of the municipality.

Require complete documentation in the disbursement voucher of LGU funds so as not to cause delay in the processing and recording of the same in the accounting office. Implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the auditor to give ample time for review.

Likewise, The Officer-In-Charge of the Treasurer’s and accounting Office is advised to follow strictly the submission of monthly accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month prescribed under Sections 100 and 107 of auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 6.04 of COA Circular No. 95-006 in order for the auditor to render the necessary decisions for the financial transactions made by the city government.
observations.managementAction:
Meeting was conducted by the Management with the Accounting staffs to discuss reasons and solutions of the issues.

The Accounting Department was directed to submit the accounts, reports of collection and/or disbursement and other required financial reports and statements but failed to submit them within the reglementary period due to the lack of staffs and delays in submission of vouchers to the Accounting office. Additional staff have been hired to rectify the delays

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the review of the related accounting entries/journal of entry vouchers to locate the source of errors so that appropriate adjustments can be effected.
observations.managementAction: A new Municipal Accountant was recently hired and was assigned to review the accunts.
observations.reason: Lack of human resource or staff  in Accounting  office

observations.recommendation: We have recommended requiring all concerned municipal officials and employees to exert efforts to collect these receivables and make sure that receivable accounts taken up in the books are properly and adequately documented.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office was adviced to send letters/statement of accounts to collect the outstanding receivables as stated in the current Financial Statement. Efforts have been made since the previous years.
observations.reason: Receivables of currently employed staffs were all paid; but some old receivables of staffs who are no longer employed by the LGU are still not paid.

observations.recommendation: In order to ascertain the propriety, validity and legality of cash account, we have recommended preparation and submission of   bank reconciliation statements for review and verification.
observations.managementAction: Bank reconciliation Statements were made but were delayed.  The management   hired an additional accounting staff to prepare BRS on time.
observations.reason: BRS were prepared after the receipt of the bank statements, which were usually late, therefore, causing the delay.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended compliance of COA Circular No. 2009-001 requiring that copies of contracts and purchase orders together with all the supporting documents shall be furnished to the Auditor for review.
observations.managementAction: The attentions of the concerned offices were called. The BAC was required by the management to submit purchase orders and not                             only contracts for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office should halt the accounting practice of treating the  supplies as an outright expense because financial statements will not generate accurate information on inventories and its corresponding expense accounts, thereby, misleading to the readers of financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The supplies were minimal and, after the purchase, these were checked and verfied by BAC, and the concerned offices immediately get their   respective shares and nothing or minimal would usually be left in the GSO Office.

Eventually, RHU would set up a system that will monitor the stock of drugs and medicines every month. Quarterly reports of its inventory would then be submitted to GSO. As for other concerned offices, the Accounting will demand year-end inventory reports of supplies which will be handed-over simultaneously with the submission of Annual Procurement Plans.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning  Officer to sit down with one another and discuss the matter of discrepancy in recording in-progress infrastructure projects in order to reflect a true and accurate financial information on CIP Accounts.

The Inspectorate Team should validate by submitting reports on completed projects both under current and continuing appropriation of 20% Development Fund so as to cleanse the CIP Accounts of any completed projects.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting, the Municipal Engineering and Municipal Planning and Development offices already conducted a meeting to synchronize their    reports on Construction-in-Progress funded by the Local Development  Fund, Provincial Aid and various Government Agencies through the 'Bottom-Up-Budgeting' and other projects peculiar to the different Government Agencies or Departments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all movable assets and require the Municipal General Services Office to conduct physical count of movable properties, in order to reflect a true and accurate PPE in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accounting Office already maintains subsidiary ledgers for all movable parts preparation of these are done every end of the year. 
The Municipal GSO have conducted a physical count of movable properties only once since its creation which is a years ago.   They will now conduct an annual physical count of movable properties.
observations.reason: The Municipal GSO have conducted a physical count on movable  properties but records of the accounting were not updated.

observations.managementAction: All the staff involved in the fiscal management of the LGU conducted a   meeting and it was agreed that all concerned offices will immediately stop the Petty Cash recording system that they had been using for years and strictly follow the imprest system.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should furnish the auditor copies of all purchase orders and perfected contracts within five (5) days after execution, regardless of the mode of procurement, as clearly stipulated in Section A.1 of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee was informed to furnish the auditor of all the copies of purchase orders and  completed contracts within five (5) days after execution, regardless of the mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management ensures that identified programs and projects funded by 20% Development Fund are fully implemented within the year and to adhere consistently to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting, the Municipal Engineering & Municipal Budget Office held a meeting to discuss why only 57% of the LDF budget were spent.
observations.reason:
It was partially implemented
because the LGU needed counterpart/equity for PRDP projects. Unfortunately, the PRDP projects were    delayed and therefore the funds were not spent.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management to strictly adhere to the specific provisions declared in Philippine Commission on Women Memorandum Circular Number 2009-1 regarding the formulation of a GAD Code. Furthermore, it must ensure that concerns on gender equality, women human rights and women empowerment are addressed accordingly.
observations.managementAction: GAD Code was just formulated  by the new Focal Person this    year 2017

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management should faithfully follow the approved Annual Local School Board Budget and should refrain from using allotted cash to pay other regular expenditures to avoid occurrence of overdraft. observations.managementAction: The accounts were verified and the overdraft has been known to be a result of a human error in the preparation of the reports.
